title: Note: This is a partial recording of the first interview. Parts of the audio have been lost. However, the full transcript is available for download.
description: Arthur understood four languages as a boy, even German. His parents didn't speak English well.

title: Arthur knew four languages as a child (Swedish, Danish, Norwegian, and English)
description: Arthur understood four languages as a boy, even German. His parents didn't speak English well.

title: Arthur's father hurt while hunting; father deeds Arthur the farm upon his death; Jorgeson store and caskets
description: One side of father's face blown off while hunting wolves in Norway. Place deeded to Arthur when father died. A bargain on a good casket at Jorgeson's store. Jorgeson underpriced the other Deary store by buying by the carload. Arthur recognizes Jorgeson's boy forty years later.

title: Building the Cornell railroad; dangerous situations; murder and explosions
description: Building the grade for the W. I. M. rail road through Cornell. Italians murdered and buried along the grade. An explosion. Arthur works on a high trestle. Italians were good people, some stayed; Sam Piwash became a successful logger.
